title: To oppose the permitting of deep seabed mining and exploration for deep seabed mining, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill tells the President to have US representatives ask international organizations to stop giving permits for deep seabed mining. Congress thinks mining the deep ocean floor is dangerous for ocean animals and environments, but scientists do not know enough about these dangers yet. The US should keep opposing deep seabed mining until scientists do more research and create strong rules to protect the oceans. The President can only change this position after an international group creates science-based rules that will keep ocean environments safe.
last_action_text: Sponsor introductory remarks on measure. (CR E86-87)

sections.title: Section 1  Opposition to Permitting of Deep Seabed Mining and Exploration for Deep Seabed Mining
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts about deep seabed mining. The United Nations says our oceans are in great danger because of climate change, taking resources from them, and pollution. The United Nations created a goal in two thousand fifteen to protect and carefully use oceans, seas, and marine resources. This goal asks countries to manage ocean ecosystems so they avoid serious harm and become stronger. Scientists and policy experts around the world agree that deep seabed mining is very risky for ocean environments. It would destroy unique and important species that live nowhere else. It would create large clouds of dirt in the water that harm ecosystems far from where the mining happens. It would make noise pollution that causes stress to sea animals. It would likely contaminate fish that people eat. It would probably hurt the ocean's ability to store carbon and fight climate change. The United Nations Convention on Biological Diversity told member countries in decision fifteen dash twenty four that before allowing deep seabed mining, they must do enough research on how it affects ocean environments and wildlife. Countries must understand the risks well enough and create strong rules to make sure mining does not harm ocean environments and wildlife. A two thousand twenty two United Nations Environment Programme report said that financing deep seabed mining activities does not follow the rules for sustainable ocean economy financing. Right now there is not enough scientific information about the deep sea and ocean ecosystems to fully understand all the risks and impacts of deep seabed mining. Congress believes that no deep seabed mining should happen in international ocean areas unless the International Seabed Authority creates a complete and legally binding set of rules following the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, especially Article one hundred forty five. There is not enough scientific understanding right now and no agreement about how much risk deep seabed mining poses to oceans and related ecosystems. The International Seabed Authority should only create rules based on complete scientific understanding and agreement about the potential risks and impacts of deep seabed mining on ocean ecosystems and the communities and activities that depend on oceans, including fishing, indigenous peoples, and coastal communities. This complete scientific understanding and agreement should only come from having enough scientific baseline information about all the impacts of deep seabed mining on ocean and related ecosystems. No deep seabed mining should be allowed in international ocean areas unless it is done in a way and under clear and enforceable rules that effectively protect ocean environments from harmful effects, do not pose risks to communities that depend on ocean ecosystems, and prevent damage to ocean plants and animals. This must be consistent with the obligations in Article one hundred forty five of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea and related global commitments for protecting and sustainably using ocean environments. The President must direct the United States representative to each relevant international organization where the United States is a member or observer to use America's voice, vote, and influence in those organizations to call for a stop to permitting deep seabed mining and exploration for deep seabed mining, and to oppose investments in or other financing or support of deep seabed mining and exploration for deep seabed mining. The President must do this until the President gives Congress both a certification and a report. The certification must state that deep seabed mining regulations have been created by the International Seabed Authority and that these regulations are based on scientific agreement about impacts to ocean ecosystems and will ensure effective protection of ocean environments from harmful effects of deep seabed mining. The report must include details of the deep seabed mining regulations created by the International Seabed Authority, the scientific agreement on risks and impacts of these regulations on ocean ecosystems and communities that depend on ocean ecosystems, and the methods by which these regulations ensure effective protection of ocean environments from harmful effects of deep seabed mining.
